app.name: Summarize
app.one_liner: Fast summaries from URLs, files, and media via CLI and browser extensions with AI-powered, media-aware processing.

screens.name: Chrome Side Panel / Firefox Sidebar
screens.purpose: Display streaming AI summary for the current tab or uploaded media/file, with chat history and model controls.

rust_commands.name: summarize_daemon_install
rust_commands.purpose: Install and configure the local daemon with token authentication for secure localhost communication with browser extension.

rust_commands.name: summarize_refresh_free
rust_commands.purpose: Configure free-tier model access via OpenRouter using OPENROUTER_API_KEY and optionally set as default.

rust_commands.name: summarize_slides
rust_commands.purpose: Extract timestamped slide cards with OCR from local video files or YouTube URLs.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build localhost daemon with token auth
week 1: Create Chrome Side Panel UI with streaming output
week 1: Implement core CLI with URL/media ingestion
week 1: Integrate first LLM backend (e.g., OpenAI-compatible)
acceptance_tests: After installing the extension and daemon with a token, the Side Panel successfully communicates with the localhost service without exposing it to external networks.
Given a local video file, running `summarize slides <file>` produces timestamped OCR-enhanced slide cards routed through the shared slide-aware flow.
Given a YouTube URL, the system extracts published transcript, falls back to Groq/AssemblyAI if needed, generates slide cards with OCR, and returns a streaming Markdown summary.
When navigating between URLs in the same tab, chat history remains isolated per URL (note: currently failing per E-IS-001).
